ULR-34: Implement review actions and thread state updates from the chat workspace
description:
Area: Review Flow
Suggested labels: backend, frontend, threads, review

Objective:
Let users request changes or approve directly from the chat-plus-terminal workflow while keeping the same thread as the durable review and execution object.

Scope:

- Implement `threads.request_changes` and `threads.approve`
- Emit review and completion events, update thread snapshots, and surface review-state feedback in the chat and thread panes
- Ensure request-changes and approval actions resolve back into the selected thread coordinator context rather than spawning a new execution object

Acceptance Criteria:

- Requesting changes returns the same thread to `running`
- Approval marks the thread complete according to the product contract
- Review actions are thread-aware and operate against the currently selected sandbox and thread context
- The thread remains the primary control surface for execution follow-up even when review actions are triggered from the main chat

Doc References:

- docs/product-spec.md
- docs/thread-contract.md
- docs/wiring/editor-review-wiring.md

title: ULR-35: Add M3 sandbox terminal hardening tests
ULR-47: Implement external handoff actions from the active sandbox context
ULR-50: Implement artifact bundle storage and large-payload staging
ULR-53: Implement runtime artifact capture services
ULR-54: Build share-to-chat/thread flows and transcript rendering
ULR-55: Implement Share All Context and oversized-bundle summarization
ULR-57: Add M5 handoff and artifact sharing tests

ULR-25: Implement thread snapshot/event schema and creation/promotion transactions
ULR-77: Add artifacts and sharing migrations
ULR-26: Build thread list, detail surface, and replayable coordinator context
description:
Area: Thread UI / Streaming
Suggested labels: frontend, backend, threads, streaming

Objective:
Make the right side of the Chat page a real execution surface centered on one coordinator conversation per thread rather than a placeholder or raw swarm dashboard.

Scope:

- Build collapsible thread cards for the active project/chat scope, with one expanded thread at a time by default
- Build thread detail with header, status pills, summary, coordinator conversation, timeline, and secondary tabs for `Overview`, `Agents`, `Files`, `Approvals`, and `Logs`
- Implement `threads.list_by_project/list_by_chat/get/get_events/get_messages/get_agents/get_approvals/get_logs`, `threads.send_message`, and replayable `threads.events/messages`
- Wire thread-driven terminal launch: thread detail UI should expose an `Open Terminal` action that calls `sandboxes.set_active` with the thread's sandbox, then `terminal.open` to launch a session targeting that sandbox. Consume the terminal workflow module built in ULR-31 rather than reimplementing.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Thread cards update from snapshots and thread detail rehydrates from persisted data
- Thread events can replay from `from_sequence` after reconnect
- The main interaction surface of a thread is one durable coordinator conversation
- Worker or swarm detail remains secondary under `Agents` or `Logs`
- `Open Terminal` from thread UI focuses the terminal drawer targeting the thread's sandbox

Implementation Notes:

- Keep list queries snapshot-based and timeline/history stream-based
- Thread-driven terminal launch should use the `openTerminal` workflow from ULR-31 and pass the thread's sandboxId for sandbox resolution
- The user talks to the thread coordinator, not directly to raw workers; provider details may be shown as diagnostics but should not replace the coordinator as the stable identity

Doc References:

- docs/thread-contract.md
- docs/ui-layout-and-navigation.md
- docs/wiring/chat-thread-wiring.md

title: ULR-38: Build generic runtime supervisor with restart policy
description:
Area: Runtime Supervision
Suggested labels: backend, runtime, supervisor, processes

Objective:
Create the backend-owned supervision core that starts, monitors, restarts, and degrades runtime components while preserving Ultra's product boundary around Overstory.

Scope:

- Build a generic supervisor for coordinator, `ov watch`, and watchdog child processes
- Implement the documented restart/backoff policy and exhaustion-to-degraded behavior
- Keep runtime supervision behind the coordinator boundary so Ultra continues to expose one coordinator conversation per thread rather than raw worker orchestration

Acceptance Criteria:

- Failed components restart according to the documented bounded policy
- Restart-count and failure-reason state persist and propagate to health views
- Restart-loop exhaustion becomes explicit degraded/down state
- The supervision layer does not expose raw worker-level control as a primary UX surface

Implementation Notes:

- Keep process ownership with Ultra; do not blur worker-level orchestration into this layer
- This ticket is infrastructure for `ov watch`, project coordinators, and watchdogs rather than a user-facing runtime UI ticket

Doc References:

- docs/coordinator-runtime.md
- docs/implementation-plan/04-runtime-supervision-architecture.md

title: ULR-39: Supervise global ov watch
description:
Area: Runtime / ov watch
Suggested labels: backend, runtime, ov-watch

Objective:
Implement the one-global-`ov watch` model the product assumes without surfacing `ov watch` as a primary user-facing concept.

Scope:

- Add a service that ensures exactly one machine-level `ov watch` process exists
- Persist and expose its health independently from project-scoped coordinators
- Integrate `ov watch` supervision into the backend-owned runtime control plane while keeping thread UX centered on coordinator conversations and thread state

Acceptance Criteria:

- The backend enforces single-instance behavior for `ov watch`
- `ov watch` health can be queried and subscribed to
- Failure and recovery updates show up in runtime status surfaces
- `ov watch` remains visible as infrastructure health, not as a direct conversational or control surface

Implementation Notes:

- Keep `project_id` null for the global component record

Doc References:

- docs/coordinator-runtime.md
- docs/implementation-plan/04-runtime-supervision-sprint-plan.md

Note: ov watch concept retired as of 2026-03-19. Replaced by Ultra's internal AgentHealthMonitor. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-19-agent-orchestration-layer-design.md.

title: ULR-40: Implement project coordinator lifecycle and thread routing
description:
Area: Runtime / Orchestration
Objective: Implement OrchestrationService with internal agent lifecycle and thread routing. Ultra's backend becomes the coordinator directly, replacing the external Overstory coordinator process. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-19-agent-orchestration-layer-design.md for the full design.

title: ULR-A1: Extend runtimeComponentTypeSchema for agent processes
description: Add 'agent' to runtimeComponentTypeSchema in packages/shared/src/contracts/runtime.ts. Required for SupervisedProcessAdapter.spawn() to accept agent process specs.

title: ULR-A2: Implement orchestration error types
description: Create OrchestrationError, WorktreeError, MergeError, AgentError in apps/backend/src/orchestration/orchestration-errors.ts. Port error patterns from vendor/overstory/src/errors.ts.

title: ULR-A3: Implement WorktreeManager
description: Port git worktree lifecycle from vendor/overstory/src/worktree/manager.ts. Create/remove/list/rollback worktrees for agent isolation. Adapt Bun.spawn to Node child_process. Branch naming: ultra/{threadId}/{agentType}-{agentId}.

title: ULR-A4: Implement guard rules and AgentContainment hooks deployer
description: Port from vendor/overstory/src/agents/guard-rules.ts and hooks-deployer.ts. Deploy Claude Code hooks to agent worktrees via settings.local.json merge. Path boundary enforcement, danger guards, capability-based restrictions per agent type (lead/builder/scout/reviewer). ENV_GUARD pattern for ULTRA_AGENT=true.

title: ULR-A5: Implement NDJSON stdout parser and AgentRegistry
description: NDJSON parser: parse agent stdout lines, route recognized events (status, spawn_agent, agent_message, agent_done), treat non-JSON as log output. AgentRegistry: in-memory agent tree tracking per thread with unified state machine (pending→spawning→booting→running→completing→completed, stalled→zombie→terminated). Active count enforcement (max 8 per thread).

title: ULR-A6: Implement OverlayGenerator and agent base definitions
description: Port from vendor/overstory/src/agents/overlay.ts and agents/*.md. Two-layer instruction system: base definition (HOW) + task overlay (WHAT). Generate CLAUDE.md per agent at spawn time. Adapt agent definitions for Ultra (stdout NDJSON instead of ov mail, spawn requests instead of ov sling). Four agent types: lead, builder, scout, reviewer.

title: ULR-A7: Implement MergeResolver (tiers 1-4)
description: Port from vendor/overstory/src/merge/resolver.ts. Tier 1: clean merge with pre-flight checks (dirty tree detection, auto-stash, untracked file handling). Tier 2: auto-resolve with hasContentfulCanonical safety check. Tier 3: AI-resolve via provider-agnostic callback with looksLikeProse validation. Tier 4: reimagine (abort, fetch both versions, full reimplementation). FIFO merge queue per thread.

title: ULR-A8: Implement AgentHealthMonitor (ZFC watchdog)
description: Port from vendor/overstory/src/watchdog/daemon.ts and health.ts. ZFC principle: observable state > recorded state. PID liveness via process.kill(pid, 0). Progressive escalation: warn→nudge→triage→terminate. Operates within unified agent state machine. Special cases: leads waiting for sub-agents, long tool calls.

title: ULR-A9: Implement OrchestrationService
description: Central glue service replacing CoordinatorService. Wires WorktreeManager, MergeResolver, AgentContainment, AgentHealthMonitor, AgentRegistry, OverlayGenerator. Core methods: startThread, handleAgentEvent, spawnSubAgent, completeAgent, terminateAgent. Spawns agents directly via SupervisedProcessAdapter (not RuntimeSupervisor). NDJSON stdout parsing for agent events.

title: ULR-A10: Wire OrchestrationService into backend and retire CoordinatorService
description: Replace CoordinatorService with OrchestrationService in apps/backend/src/index.ts. Remove WatchService (ov watch retired). Update thread dispatch handler. Remove watchService from startSocketServer arguments.

title: ULR-A11: Implement provider model mapping
description: Provider-to-model mapping for agent spawning. Claude: opus/sonnet/haiku. OpenAI: o3/gpt-4o/gpt-4o-mini. Google: gemini-pro/gemini-flash. Keyed by chat provider, user-overridable.

title: ULR-A12: Add secret redaction utility
description: Port from vendor/overstory/src/logging/sanitizer.ts. Regex patterns for redacting sk-ant-*, github_pat_*, ghp_*, Bearer tokens, API keys from agent output. Applied to all agent stdout before thread event projection.

title: ULR-A13: Update specs and docs for internal orchestration
description: Retire coordinator-runtime.md (deprecation header). Update product-spec.md, thread-contract.md, thread-event-schema.md to replace Overstory references. Update source values from ov.* to ultra.*. Deprecate ov-prefixed identity fields.

title: ULR-A14: Rewrite M4 runtime supervision architecture and sprint plan
description: Rewrite docs/implementation-plan/04-runtime-supervision*.md around internal orchestration. New milestone scope, architecture around OrchestrationService, updated sprint plan with component tickets.
